title: राधा चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
श्री राधा चरणन सरोज, मन में धरि ध्यान।
वृषभानु सुता जगत मात, कहूँ चालीसा गान॥
श्री कृष्ण प्रिया राधिके, तुम हो प्रेम अवतार।
करो कृपा अब दासन पर, हरो जगत दुख भार॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय राधा वृषभानु दुलारी।
श्री कृष्ण प्राणप्रिया सुखकारी॥
वृषभानु राय के घर जन्मीं।
कीर्ति दा माता सुत सुखरूमीं॥
बरसाना धाम तिहारा।
जहँ निवास करत सुखसारा॥
गोरा वरन मनोहर रूपा।
जगमग ज्योति अनूप अनूपा॥
नीला वसन सोहे तन ऊपर।
कमल नयन मुख चन्द्र मनोहर॥
मन्द मुस्कान अधर सोहे।
त्रिभुवन जन के मन को मोहे॥
कुण्डल मकर श्रवण में सोहे।
नासिका अग्र मोती जो मोहे॥
केश काले घने लम्बे सुन्दर।
वेणी फूल गूँथे अति मनहर॥
माथे बिन्दिया सिन्दूर विराजे।
पायल छनक पद कमल में साजे॥
कंगना बाजूबन्द विराजे।
हाथन में मेंहदी रचि साजे॥
गोपियन संग रास रचावे।
श्री कृष्ण संग वन में जावे॥
यमुना तट पर केलि करावें।
कदम्ब तरु तले बंसी बजावें॥
निधिवन कुंज में रास विलासा।
राधा कृष्ण का प्रेम प्रकाशा॥
गोपियन को प्रेम सिखावे।
भक्तन को प्रभु दर्श करावे॥
लीला रस की स्वामिनी तुम हो।
प्रेम भक्ति की दानिनी तुम हो॥
तुम बिन कृष्ण अधूरे रहते।
तुम बिन जगत न कछु कहते॥
श्रीमद्भागवत कथा सुहानी।
राधा कृष्ण प्रेम की बानी॥
चन्द्रावली से प्रीत निभावे।
ललिता विशाखा संग सुहावे॥
ब्रह्मा शिव शेष सब ध्यावें।
राधा पद रज शीश चढ़ावें॥
जो जन राधा शरण में आवे।
सो सब सुख सम्पदा पावे॥
प्रेम भक्ति का वर जो माँगे।
राधा कृपा से सब सुख पावे॥
कृष्ण कृपा बिन कछु न होई।
राधा कृपा बिन कृष्ण न कोई॥
राधा नाम जपत सुखदाई।
कृष्ण मिलन का पथ बतलाई॥
सकल मनोरथ पूरन होवें।
जो श्री राधा चालीसा बोवें॥
प्रेम के अंकुर हृदय में जागें।
राधा कृपा से सब दुख भागें॥
दम्पति प्रेम सदा बढ़ जावे।
राधा कृष्ण भजन जो गावे॥
संसार सागर से पार लगावे।
वैकुण्ठ धाम को पथ बतलावे॥
जय राधा रानी वृन्दावन वासी।
कृपा करो दीनन पर दासी॥
चालीसा राधा का गाये।
सो परम पद निश्चय पाये॥

॥ दोहा ॥
राधे राधे जपत रहो, मन में धरो विश्वास।
कृष्ण मिलन हो निश्चय, राधा करे प्रकाश॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Shri Radha Charanan Saroj, Man Mein Dhari Dhyan.
Vrishabhanu Suta Jagat Maat, Kahun Chalisa Gaan.
Shri Krishna Priya Radhike, Tum Ho Prem Avtar.
Karo Kripa Ab Dasan Par, Haro Jagat Dukh Bhar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Jai Radha Vrishabhanu Dulari.
Shri Krishna Pranpriya Sukhkari.
Vrishabhanu Rai Ke Ghar Janmi.
Kirti Da Mata Sut Sukhrumi.
Barsana Dham Tihara.
Jahan Nivas Karat Sukhsara.
Gora Varan Manohar Roopa.
Jagmag Jyoti Anoop Anoopa.
Neela Vasan Sohe Tan Oopar.
Kamal Nayan Mukh Chandra Manohar.
Mand Muskan Adhar Sohe.
Tribhuvan Jan Ke Man Ko Mohe.
Kundal Makar Shravan Mein Sohe.
Nasika Agra Moti Jo Mohe.
Kesh Kale Ghane Lambe Sundar.
Veni Phool Gunthe Ati Manhar.
Mathe Bindiya Sindoor Viraje.
Payal Chhanak Pad Kamal Mein Saje.
Kangna Bajuband Viraje.
Hathan Mein Mehndi Rachi Saje.
Gopiyan Sang Ras Rachave.
Shri Krishna Sang Van Mein Jave.
Yamuna Tat Par Keli Karaven.
Kadamb Taru Tale Bansi Bajaven.
Nidhivan Kunj Mein Ras Vilasa.
Radha Krishna Ka Prem Prakasha.
Gopiyan Ko Prem Sikhave.
Bhaktan Ko Prabhu Darsh Karave.
Leela Ras Ki Swamini Tum Ho.
Prem Bhakti Ki Danini Tum Ho.
Tum Bin Krishna Adhoore Rehte.
Tum Bin Jagat Na Kachhu Kehte.
Shrimadbhagavat Katha Suhani.
Radha Krishna Prem Ki Bani.
Chandravali Se Preet Nibhave.
Lalita Vishakha Sang Suhave.
Brahma Shiv Shesh Sab Dhyaven.
Radha Pad Raj Shish Chadhaven.
Jo Jan Radha Sharan Mein Ave.
So Sab Sukh Sampada Pave.
Prem Bhakti Ka Var Jo Mange.
Radha Kripa Se Sab Sukh Pave.
Krishna Kripa Bin Kachhu Na Hoi.
Radha Kripa Bin Krishna Na Koi.
Radha Naam Japat Sukhdai.
Krishna Milan Ka Path Batlai.
Sakal Manorath Puran Hove.
Jo Shri Radha Chalisa Bove.
Prem Ke Ankur Hriday Mein Jage.
Radha Kripa Se Sab Dukh Bhage.
Dampati Prem Sada Badh Jave.
Radha Krishna Bhajan Jo Gave.
Sansar Sagar Se Par Lagave.
Vaikunth Dham Ko Path Batlave.
Jai Radha Rani Vrindavan Vasi.
Kripa Karo Dinan Par Dasi.
Chalisa Radha Ka Gaye.
So Param Pad Nishchay Paye.

|| Doha ||
Radhe Radhe Japat Raho, Man Mein Dharo Vishvas.
Krishna Milan Ho Nishchay, Radha Kare Prakash.
hindiMeaning:
श्री राधा के चरण कमलों में ध्यान धरकर, वृषभानु की पुत्री जगत माता की चालीसा का गान करता हूँ। श्री कृष्ण की प्रियतमा राधिके! तुम प्रेम का अवतार हो, अब दासों पर कृपा करो और जगत के दुखों का भार हरो।

राधा रानी वृषभानु जी की दुलारी हैं, श्री कृष्ण की प्राणप्रिया और सुखकारी हैं। बरसाना उनका पावन धाम है। उनका गोरा रूप अत्यंत मनोहर है।
benefits: Deepens devotion and love for the divine
Blesses married couples with lasting love and harmony
Invokes the grace of radha rani for krishna bhakti
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles in matters of love and relationships
Invokes the blessing of the highest form of devotion - prema bhakti
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. The Radha Chalisa, known in Sanskrit as राधा चालीसा, is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn in praise of Goddess Radha. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional Chalisa Sangrah and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to bless married couples with lasting love and harmony, deepen devotion and love for the divine, and bestow the highest form of devotion - Prema Bhakti. It is especially recommended on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Radha Ashtami, Holi, and Janmashtami for maximum spiritual benefit. With 42 verses and a reading time of about 14 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Radha Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Radha Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Devotional prayer to Radha Rani, beloved of Lord Krishna.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Radha Chalisa and to whom is it dedicated?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Chalisa is a forty-verse (chalis = forty) devotional hymn in Awadhi-tinged Hindi composed in praise of Goddess Radha — the eternal beloved (pranapriya) of Lord Krishna and the supreme deity of love (prema-bhakti) in the Vaishnava tradition. The composition follows the standard chalisa structure popularised by Tulsidas's Hanuman Chalisa: two opening dohas, forty chaupais of praise and narrative, and a closing doha. It celebrates Radha's birth in Barsana to King Vrishabhanu and Queen Kirti, her divine beauty, her love-pastimes (rasa-lila) with Krishna in the kunja-vana of Vrindavan, her gopi companions Lalita-Vishakha-Chandravali, and her supreme position as the source of Krishna-prema. (Sources: Traditional Chalisa Sangrah; Vrindavan Vaishnava bhakti literature.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Radha Chalisa and when?
seoFaqs.answer: Like many devotional Chalisas in the Hindi-Awadhi tradition, the Radha Chalisa does not have a confirmed individual author and is preserved as a compilation in the broad stream of Braj-Vrindavan bhakti literature. It emerged within the milieu of the Vraja sampradaya — the Gaudiya Vaishnava (Chaitanya), Pushti Marg (Vallabha), Nimbarka and Radhavallabha lineages that flourished in Vrindavan and Mathura from the 16th century onwards and elevated Radha to the status of supreme Goddess. The Chalisa style itself was popularised by Tulsidas in the 16th century, and the Radha Chalisa likely took its current form in the centuries thereafter as Vrindavan emerged as the centre of Radha-bhakti. It circulates today in Chalisa Sangraha collections used by households across North India. (Sources: Vrindavan Vaishnava sampradaya history; Chalisa literature studies.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Radha and the city of Barsana?
seoFaqs.answer: Barsana, located about 50 km north of Mathura in present-day Uttar Pradesh, is the birthplace of Radha and the seat of her father King Vrishabhanu. The Chalisa explicitly invokes this — Vrishabhanu raya ke ghar janmi, Kirti da mata sut sukhrumi, Barsana dham tihara. Pilgrims today visit the Shriji Mandir on Brahma-Pahari hill (the temple of Larli Lal — Radha) as the principal Radha shrine, along with sites like Sankari Khor, the Maan Mandir and the Dohani Kund. Radha is therefore worshipped as Barsane Wali Radha Rani — the Queen of Barsana — and the entire Chalisa is considered an invocation of the Radha-tattva that resides in Barsana. The festival of Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami) draws lakhs of devotees to Barsana. (Sources: Skanda Purana Vraja Mahatmya; Vrindavan tirtha-yatra guides.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Radha Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The most auspicious occasions are: Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami — Radha's birth anniversary, the supreme Radha festival); Janmashtami (Krishna's birth, when Radha-Krishna are worshipped together); Holi (especially the famous Lath-mar Holi of Barsana and Phulera Dooj); the entire month of Kartik (when devotees observe the Damodara-vrata in honour of Radha-Damodara); Sharad Purnima (the night of the maha-rasa-lila); Govardhan Puja and Annakut; every Wednesday and Friday (the days associated with Krishna and the Devi forms); Ekadashi tithis; and the four months of chaturmas. Daily recitation is particularly powerful for those undertaking Radha-bhakti sadhana, with the early morning sandhya being the ideal time. (Sources: drikpanchang.com Radha Ashtami; Vraja-bhakti calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Radha Chalisa promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chalisa promises multiple benefits to sincere reciters: deepening of prema-bhakti (the highest form of devotion) toward Krishna, since Radha is regarded as the very embodiment of Krishna-prema; lasting love and harmony between married couples (dampati prem sada badh jave); removal of obstacles in matters of love, romance and relationships; fulfilment of all wholesome desires (sakal manorath puran hove); awakening of seeds of pure love in the heart (prem ke ankur hriday mein jagen); removal of all sorrow through Radha's grace (Radha kripa se sab dukh bhagen); and ultimately the path to Vaikuntha and union with Krishna. The Vraja sampradaya teaches that no devotee can approach Krishna directly — only through the mediation of Radha, making the Chalisa essential for all Krishna-bhaktas. (Sources: Chalisa internal verses; Gaudiya Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does the Chalisa say Radha kripa bin Krishna na koi?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse Krishna kripa bin kachhu na hoi, Radha kripa bin Krishna na koi — without Krishna's grace nothing happens, but without Radha's grace there is no Krishna at all — is one of the most theologically significant lines in the Chalisa. It encapsulates the Vraja sampradaya's central teaching: Radha is the hladini-shakti (the bliss-giving energy) of Krishna without which Krishna himself cannot exist as the personal Lord. Acharyas like Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Vallabhacharya and Hit Harivamsh elevated this insight into a complete theology in which Radha is not merely Krishna's consort but is his other half — the active, loving aspect of the same supreme reality. The line therefore teaches the practical sadhana point that one cannot reach Krishna by bypassing Radha. (Sources: Sri Chaitanya Charitamrita; Hit Harivamsh's Hit Chaurasi; Radhavallabha philosophy.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Radha Chalisa compare with Hanuman Chalisa in style?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Chalisa follows the same structural template as Tulsidas's celebrated Hanuman Chalisa: two introductory dohas invoking the deity and stating the purpose, forty chaupais of glorification and narrative, and one or two closing dohas with phalashruti. Both compositions use the simple Awadhi-Brajbhasha vernacular accessible to ordinary householders rather than Sanskrit-only versifications, making them suitable for daily home recitation by people of all educational levels. The Radha Chalisa adapts this Tulsidas-popularised template to focus on the gentler, emotional sphere of madhurya-bhakti (sweet-love devotion) rather than the heroic veera-bhakti of Hanuman. Both Chalisas are typically recited in about 10-15 minutes and can be sung in simple dhun-style melodies. (Sources: Hindi-Awadhi Chalisa tradition; Tulsidas-influenced devotional literature.)
